L1.area: Cryptographic operations and application configuration setup

L2.description: An insufficient entropy vulnerability exists in the generation of a cryptographic secret used for encryption operations. The secret is derived from a time-based, non-cryptographic function whose output is predictable. An attacker can infer the approximate generation timestamp from publicly accessible information and brute force the remaining entropy offline, allowing them to forge valid encrypted password recovery tokens and escalate privileges to administrator.

L3.area: Cryptographic operations and application configuration setup
L3.description: An insufficient entropy vulnerability exists in the generation of a cryptographic secret used for encryption operations. The secret is derived from a time-based, non-cryptographic function whose output is predictable. An attacker can infer the approximate generation timestamp from publicly accessible information and brute force the remaining entropy offline, allowing them to forge valid encrypted password recovery tokens and escalate privileges to administrator.

ground_truth.reason: The application generates a cryptographic salt using uniqid(), a time-based function with insufficient entropy, allowing an attacker to brute force the salt offline and forge valid encrypted password recovery codes for privilege escalation.
